items.briefing:
Cybercriminals are exploiting ChatGPT's shared-link feature to create convincing fake "service outage" pages that trick users into downloading malware. If you receive a link to a ChatGPT page claiming the service is down, do not click any buttons or download anything — go directly to the official OpenAI website instead. Separately, a study has highlighted manipulative design tactics used by some AI chatbots to encourage users to share more personal information than intended, a reminder to be cautious about what you disclose to AI tools.

**Practical tip:** Always type official website addresses directly into your browser rather than clicking links shared via chat or email.

featured.title: ChatGPT share links abused to host fake outage pages to deliver malware - BleepingComputer
featured.quote: Attackers exploit trusted ChatGPT links to deliver malware, bypassing user security awareness.
